items.a1: We operate on a subscription model. Payment is taken when you subscribe. We accept all major debit and credit cards. Full details are covered during your consultation.

scienceTutoring.heroTitle: Science tutoring across Biology, Chemistry, and Physics
scienceTutoring.heroSubtitle: 1:1 science lessons matched to your child's exam board and level. The kind of explanation that actually makes the textbook make sense.

challenges.title: Common Challenges
challenges.subtitle: Most students who come to us are dealing with at least one of these. Sound familiar?

1.title: Balancing Three Subjects
1.desc: Biology, Chemistry, and Physics each have their own vocabulary, methods, and exam style. Most students find one clicks and the other two don't — yet.

2.title: Understands it, can't apply it
2.desc: Theory makes sense in class. Then the exam question rewords it, adds a graph, or asks why, and the answer doesn't come. That gap between understanding and applying is where most Science marks are lost.

3.title: Required practicals
3.desc: Schools cover them quickly and move on. But required practicals come up in exams and they need to be understood, not just memorised. Most students don't realise this until it's too late.

4.title: The GCSE-to-A-Level wall
4.desc: Triple Science GCSE and A-Level Chemistry are not the same subject. The content almost doubles, the questions go deeper, and the exam technique changes completely. Students who coasted through GCSE often hit this wall hard in Year 12.

challenges.title: Common Challenges
challenges.subtitle: Most students who come to us are dealing with at least one of these. Sound familiar?

1.title: I speak it, I just can't write it
1.desc: Native speakers often have strong spoken Arabic but haven't been formally taught to read or write it. School Arabic then feels like a foreign language — technically one they already speak.

2.title: Starting from scratch
2.desc: For students learning Arabic as a complete beginner, the alphabet alone is a significant hurdle before any vocabulary or grammar can be tackled. Progress feels slow before it suddenly feels fast.

3.title: Formal versus spoken Arabic
3.desc: The Arabic used in exams is Modern Standard Arabic, not the dialect spoken at home. Even fluent Arabic speakers have to be taught the formal register — it's a separate skill.

4.title: Falling behind in a fast-moving class
4.desc: Arabic class composition in schools varies enormously. A student surrounded by fluent speakers can fall behind quickly and lose confidence just as fast.

2.title: 3-6 months before

3.title: 1-3 months before

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions
faqs.subtitle: Everything you need to know before getting started.

1.question: How far in advance should we start exam prep tutoring?
1.answer: Earlier is always better, but there's no bad time to start. Six months gives us time to close gaps properly. Three months is enough for focused revision and past paper practice. Even four weeks of targeted support can make a real difference if the preparation is structured well.

2.question: How is exam prep tutoring different from regular subject tutoring?
2.answer: Regular subject tutoring builds understanding over time. Exam prep tutoring is focused on one outcome: performing well on a specific exam, on a specific date. Every lesson is structured around the mark scheme, the examiner's expectations, and the techniques that convert knowledge into marks. The two often overlap, but the focus is different.

3.question: Do your tutors cover all GCSE and A-Level exam boards?
3.answer: Yes. Our tutors cover AQA, Edexcel, OCR, Cambridge IGCSE, and the IB across all subjects. Many of our tutors are also examiners themselves, so they know exactly what the mark scheme requires and how answers are assessed. When we match your child with a tutor, we match on exam board specifically, not just subject.

4.question: Can you help with last-minute exam preparation?
4.answer: Yes. We've helped students make meaningful improvements in as little as four weeks. The approach is different. We focus on high-impact topics, exam technique, and confidence rather than trying to cover everything. Get in touch and we'll tell you honestly what's achievable.

5.question: How do you decide which tutor to match us with?
5.answer: We look at exam board knowledge, subject expertise, your child's specific timeline, and their target grades. For exam prep especially, getting that match right really matters, and we take care to get it right.

6.question: Can we focus on specific topics rather than the whole subject?
6.answer: Absolutely. Tell us what needs the most attention and we'll make sure the tutor focuses there from the start.

1.question: Can you help with multiple subjects?
1.answer: Yes, we can match your child with tutors for different subjects. In some cases, one tutor may be able to cover more than one subject. We'll work out the best approach with you.

2.question: Do tutors know the specific curriculum my child follows?
2.answer: Yes. When we match, we make sure the tutor knows your child's exact curriculum: British, IB, American, and others.

3.question: What if we're not sure which subject needs help?
3.answer: That's exactly what the consultation is for. We'll have a proper conversation, work out where the gaps are, and take it from there.

4.question: What age groups do you support?
4.answer: We work with students from primary age all the way through to A-Level and IB. Whether your child is in Year 3 or Year 13, we'll find the right tutor for where they're at.

5.question: Do you offer exam preparation tutoring at home?
5.answer: Yes. We offer home exam preparation tutoring across Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Sharjah, as well as online lessons. Same vetted tutors, same structured approach. Tell us your preference during the consultation.

6.question: Can you help with a subject that isn't listed here?
6.answer: Absolutely. Get in touch and tell us what you need. If we have tutors who can help, we'll let you know. If we can't, we'll tell you that too.

howItWorksFaqs.title: Frequently asked questions
howItWorksFaqs.subtitle: Everything you need to know before getting started.

1.q: What if my child has never had tutoring before?
1.a: Most students settle in within a lesson or two. Online sessions are interactive and well paced, and home sessions feel like a natural extension of school. Either way, our tutors know how to put children at ease. It quickly starts to feel completely normal.

2.q: How do I know if the tutor you match is right?
2.a: We match based on things that genuinely lead to results: subject knowledge, teaching style, curriculum experience, and your child's specific needs. That said, if it's not clicking, we'll assign someone new.

3.q: Can I sit in on lessons?
3.a: Absolutely. Parents are welcome to observe any lesson. Just let us know in advance so the tutor can plan accordingly.

4.q: What happens if we need to change lesson times?
4.a: Just get in touch and we'll sort it out with the tutor. All scheduling goes through us, so you don't need to contact your tutor directly.

5.q: Do you match based on curriculum?
5.a: Yes. We make sure your child's tutor knows their exact curriculum, whether that's British, IB, American, or something else.

6.q: How do I know what's happening in my child's lessons?
6.a: You'll receive a written summary after every lesson covering what was covered, how your child got on, and what the tutor plans to focus on next. We also do monthly progress reviews so you always have a clear picture of how things are going.

0.question: How do you match tutors for primary students?
0.answer: We look for tutors who are experienced with younger learners: patient, encouraging, and great at making lessons feel fun rather than like extra school. We match based on your child's personality as much as anything else.

1.question: What's the right amount of tutoring for primary students?
1.answer: One to two lessons a week tends to work really well at primary age. Consistency matters more than cramming. Regular sessions keep things ticking along nicely.

2.question: Can tutoring help if my child is ahead or behind?
2.answer: Yes, for both. Whether your child needs to catch up or wants to push further ahead, we'll match them with a tutor who works at the right pace.

3.question: My child gets distracted easily. Will online lessons work?
3.answer: Our tutors are well used to keeping younger learners engaged. They use a mix of interactive tools, visual aids, and varied activities to hold attention throughout the session.

4.question: Do you assign different tutors for different subjects?
4.answer: Many of our primary tutors cover more than one subject, so that's often not necessary. We'll work out what makes most sense for your child.

5.question: Do you offer 11-plus preparation?
5.answer: Yes. We can match your child with a tutor who knows the 11-plus format, whether that's verbal reasoning, non-verbal reasoning, Maths, or English. Tell us which schools you're targeting and we'll factor that into the match.

items.question: How do you match tutors for KS3 students?
items.answer: We look for tutors who know the KS3 curriculum well and understand what the jump from primary to secondary feels like for students. Teaching style and your child's specific challenges both factor into the match.

items.question: What's the right amount of tutoring for KS3?
items.answer: One to two lessons a week is usually ideal. If your child is finding several subjects tricky, two or three lessons a week across those subjects can make a real difference.

items.question: Is KS3 too early to start tutoring?
items.answer: Not at all. It's actually a great time to start. KS3 lays the groundwork for GCSEs, so getting on top of things now means your child heads into exam years feeling confident rather than catching up.

items.question: Can tutoring help with organisation and study skills?
items.answer: Yes. A good tutor doesn't just cover the subject. They help your child develop habits and skills that make managing secondary school a lot less overwhelming.

items.question: Do you assign different tutors for different subjects?
items.answer: We can, yes. Some of our KS3 tutors cover multiple subjects; others specialise. We'll work out what suits your child best.

items.question: My child is struggling across most subjects. Where do we start?
items.answer: Start with the consultation and we'll help you prioritise. Usually it makes sense to focus on one or two subjects first and build from there, rather than trying to tackle everything at once.

items.question: How do you match tutors for GCSE students?
items.answer: We look at exam board knowledge, experience with GCSE students, teaching approach under exam pressure, and your child's target grades and timeline. Getting that combination right makes a real difference.

items.question: What's the right amount of tutoring for GCSE?
items.answer: Most GCSE students benefit from weekly lessons throughout the year, increasing to 2-3 lessons per week in the final months before exams.

items.question: When should we start GCSE tutoring?
items.answer: The earlier the better, ideally at the start of Year 10 or early Year 11. That said, even a concentrated push in the months before exams can make a real difference.

items.question: Can tutoring really improve grades?
items.answer: Consistently, yes. With the right tutor, most students improve by one or two grades. Some improve more.

items.question: What if my child needs help with multiple GCSE subjects?
items.answer: We can match tutors for different subjects, or find one tutor who covers several if that's a better fit. We'll figure out the right approach during your consultation.

items.question: Do your tutors know specific GCSE exam boards?
items.answer: Yes. We cover AQA, Edexcel, OCR, and Cambridge IGCSE across all subjects. When we match your child with a tutor, we match on their specific exam board, not just the subject.

items.question: How do you match tutors for IGCSE students?
items.answer: We look at exam board knowledge (Cambridge, Edexcel, AQA), experience with IGCSE specifications, teaching approach under exam pressure, and your child's target grades and timeline. Getting that combination right makes a real difference.

items.question: What's the right amount of tutoring for IGCSE?
items.answer: Most students benefit from one lesson per week throughout Year 10 and 11. As exams approach, many increase to two or three sessions per week for more intensive preparation. We'll recommend what makes sense based on your child's timeline and goals.

items.question: When should we start IGCSE tutoring?
items.answer: Earlier is always better, but it's never too late. Starting in Year 10 allows time to build strong foundations gradually. Starting closer to exams means a more focused, intensive approach. Either way, we'll make the time count.

items.question: What's the difference between IGCSE and GCSE tutoring?
items.answer: IGCSE syllabuses have different content, command words, and exam styles compared to standard GCSEs. Our tutors specialise in international specifications and know exactly what Cambridge, Edexcel, and AQA international exam boards expect — it's not the same as general subject tutoring.

items.question: Do your tutors cover all IGCSE exam boards?
items.answer: Yes. We cover Cambridge IGCSE, Edexcel International GCSE, AQA IGCSE, and Oxford AQA International GCSE across all subjects. If you're unsure about your exam board, get in touch and we'll confirm.

items.question: Can tutoring really improve IGCSE grades?
items.answer: Yes. With the right tutor, consistent lessons, and focused exam preparation, most students improve by one to two grades. Some improve more. The key is starting with a clear target and a tutor who knows exactly how to get there.

items.question: What if my child needs help with multiple IGCSE subjects?
items.answer: Not a problem. We can match your child with different specialist tutors for each subject and help coordinate a schedule that works around school and exam dates.

items.question: How do you match tutors for A-Level students?
items.answer: We look for tutors with strong subject knowledge at degree level or above, solid experience with A-Level specifications and exam boards, and a clear understanding of what your child needs to achieve their university goals.

items.question: What's the right amount of tutoring for A-Level?
items.answer: One to two lessons a week per subject is usually the right rhythm. For particularly tough subjects or in the run-up to exams, pushing to two or three sessions a week can make a real difference.

items.question: When should we start A-Level tutoring?
items.answer: Ideally early in Year 12. That way there's time to build strong foundations before things get intense. Year 13 support, even if it's more last-minute, can still have a significant impact.

items.question: Can tutors help with university applications?
items.answer: Academic support is our main focus, but many of our tutors are happy to give guidance on personal statements and subject knowledge for interviews as well.

items.question: What if my child is struggling with the step up from GCSE?
items.answer: It's really common, and nothing to worry about. We match tutors who understand that transition well and can help fill gaps while building the independent study habits A-Level demands.

items.question: My child is resitting their A-Levels. Can you help?
items.answer: Yes. We work with resit students regularly. Tell us what went wrong the first time and what your child needs to achieve, and we'll match them with a tutor who has experience in exactly that situation.

items.question: Why should I trust you to match the tutor instead of choosing myself?
items.answer: We've matched thousands of students, and we've learned a lot about what makes a good fit and what doesn't. You're getting the benefit of all of that experience, without having to spend hours comparing profiles yourself. And if the match isn't right, we'll sort it out.

items.question: How involved can I be?

items.question: How do you know what my child needs?

items.question: What if my child is reluctant about tutoring?
items.answer: It's more common than you might think. A good tutor makes a real difference here. We match based on personality as well as subject knowledge, and most students come around quickly once they've had a lesson that actually feels helpful rather than like more school.

items.question: What is Hello Tutor and how does it work?
items.answer: Hello Tutor handles everything. You tell us what you need, and we find the right tutor for you, arrange your lessons, and manage payment — all through the platform at app.hellotutor.me. There is no browsing, no negotiating, and no admin on your part. You just show up and learn.

items.question: Is Hello Tutor a marketplace where I choose my own tutor?
items.answer: No. Hello Tutor is not a marketplace. You share your requirements with us, and we find the right tutor, arrange your bookings, and handle payment on your behalf.

items.question: Is it free to register?
items.answer: Yes. Creating an account is free and there is no obligation to book once you have registered. You only pay once you subscribe and confirm your first lesson.

items.question: Who can register on the platform?
items.answer: You must be at least 18 years old to register and book lessons. If the student is under 18, a parent or legal guardian must create the account and provide consent for the child to receive tuition.

items.question: Where is Hello Tutor based?
items.answer: Hello Tutor is a trading name of Northstar Connect FZ-LLC, a company registered in RAKEZ, United Arab Emirates (Licence No. 47033173). We provide tutoring services delivered both online via our platform at app.hellotutor.me and in-person at the student’s home where applicable. Our Terms and Conditions and Safeguarding Policy are governed by UAE law.

items.question: How does Hello Tutor match me with a tutor?
items.answer: When you register, we gather information about your learning needs, subject, level, and availability. From there, Hello Tutor takes care of everything — finding the right tutor, arranging your bookings, and handling payment on your behalf. You do not need to negotiate scheduling or rates with anyone.

items.question: What if the match does not feel right?
items.answer: While we take care to match you with the right tutor from the outset, if a match does not work out as expected, please get in touch and we will work to find a tutor who is a stronger fit for your needs. Reach us at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How are tutors vetted before being approved?
items.answer:
Before any tutor's profile goes live, Hello Tutor carries out a thorough verification process:
  • Identity verification: government-issued photo ID and relevant qualifications are reviewed.
  • Background check: all tutors must hold a valid background check dated within the last three years. Tutors whose checks lapse are suspended until the requirement is met.
  • Written references: two written references from non-family members are required.
  • UAE work or freelance permit: non-UAE national tutors must hold a valid MOHRE permit or equivalent free zone authority permit.
If you have a concern about a tutor, contact us before proceeding with a lesson.

items.question: Are tutors employed by Hello Tutor?
items.answer: No. Tutors on Hello Tutor are independent contractors, not Hello Tutor employees. When you confirm your first booking, you enter into a direct contract with your tutor for the delivery of lessons. Hello Tutor facilitates the match and provides the booking and payment infrastructure.

items.question: Should I also verify my tutor's credentials myself?
items.answer: While Hello Tutor carries out checks on all tutors before approving them, you are also responsible for using your own judgement about a tutor's suitability. We recommend verifying any qualifications or experience claims before or at the start of your first lesson.

items.question: How do I confirm my first booking?
items.answer: Once Hello Tutor has matched you with a tutor, you will confirm your first booking through the platform. You must have valid payment details saved on your account to do so.

items.question: What happens after I confirm my first booking?
items.answer: By confirming your first booking, you authorise Hello Tutor to arrange further bookings on your behalf automatically and to take payment for each lesson. You will be notified of every booking by email and through the platform. Please always check that bookings are correct.

items.question: Do I arrange lessons directly with my tutor?
items.answer: No. All bookings are arranged by Hello Tutor on your behalf. You must not contact tutors to arrange lessons outside the platform, and all communication must go through Hello Tutor's messaging system.

items.question: What do I need to have ready before each lesson?
items.answer: Before each lesson, make sure you have shared any relevant learning objectives with Hello Tutor, your device, webcam, microphone, and internet connection are working, your payment details are up to date, and your lesson environment is appropriate and free from distraction.

items.question: Are lessons recorded?
items.answer:
Yes. All lessons are recorded by Hello Tutor for safeguarding and quality purposes. Recordings are stored for a minimum of 28 days and a maximum of one year, and remain Hello Tutor's property. They may be reviewed internally, shared with UAE law enforcement if required, or shared with students where appropriate.

Students must not independently record lessons by any means outside Hello Tutor's own recording system.

items.question: What if my tutor does not show up?
items.answer: If your tutor does not join the Online Classroom within the first 15 minutes of the scheduled start time, you will not be charged. If your tutor cancels a confirmed booking, you will receive a full refund. Please raise a complaint within 96 hours of the scheduled lesson end time.

items.question: What if I miss my lesson?
items.answer: If you do not attend a lesson, the full lesson fee will still be charged. Bookings cannot be cancelled once a lesson has started. If you are unable to attend due to exceptional circumstances, contact us and a refund may be considered at Hello Tutor's sole discretion.

items.question: Can my tutor complete my coursework or essays for me?
items.answer: No. Tutors are strictly prohibited from completing or intending to complete coursework, essays, assignments, or similar academic work on a student's behalf. Any tutor found in breach of this may be removed from the platform.

items.question: Can I leave a review for my tutor?
items.answer: Yes. After your lessons you can leave a review, comment, or rating for your tutor through the platform. Reviews are from verified students only, help maintain quality across the platform, and contribute to how tutors are ranked in our system.

items.question: How is pricing structured?
items.answer: Hello Tutor operates on a subscription model. Your lesson fee is determined by the subject, education level, and number of lessons included in your subscription. All fees are displayed before you subscribe and include any applicable UAE taxes.

items.question: What does the lesson fee include?
items.answer: Your lesson fee is made up of the Tutor Fee (the portion paid to your tutor) and Hello Tutor's Service Fee (which covers matching, booking, and platform services). The Service Fee is included within the lesson fee and is not an additional charge.

items.question: When am I charged for a lesson?
items.answer: Payment is taken at the point of subscribing. All fees are displayed clearly before you confirm your subscription.

items.question: What payment methods are accepted?
items.answer: We accept major debit and credit cards. Payments are processed securely by our third-party payment provider. Hello Tutor does not store your full card details on its own servers.

items.question: What happens if a payment fails?
items.answer:
A failed payment occurs when our payment provider cannot charge your registered card.
  • 48 and 72 hours after the lesson: payment is automatically retried.
  • 96 hours after the lesson: all future bookings are cancelled until the outstanding amount is cleared.
Hello Tutor may pursue outstanding amounts for up to 90 days and reserves the right to refer unpaid debts to a third-party collection service, which may affect your credit record.

items.question: Does Hello Tutor have a referral programme?
items.answer: Yes. If you refer a friend, you will earn platform credit once they attend their first paid lesson. Credit is valid for 3 months from the date of award, cannot be exchanged for cash, and is only earned for paid lessons. Hello Tutor may cancel credit if fraudulent activity is suspected.

items.question: Can I cancel a lesson?
items.answer:
You can cancel a booking at any point before the lesson starts, but cancellation charges apply:
  • More than 24 hours before the lesson: 50% of the lesson fee is charged.
  • Within 24 hours of the lesson: 100% of the lesson fee is charged.
Cancellations must be made through the platform. Once a lesson has started it cannot be cancelled.

Missed lessons: if you do not attend without cancelling beforehand, the full lesson fee is charged.

items.question: What if my tutor cancels?
items.answer: On rare occasions, a tutor may need to cancel due to unforeseen circumstances. If this happens, Hello Tutor will be in touch to reschedule your lesson as soon as possible. If rescheduling is not possible, you will receive a full refund.

items.question: How do I raise a complaint about a lesson?
items.answer:
A complaint covers situations where the tutor's service fell below the standard you reasonably expected, or where your lesson did not take place as booked.
  • Contact Hello Tutor by chat or email within 96 hours of the scheduled lesson end time.
  • We will contact your tutor within 72 hours of receiving your complaint.
  • If the tutor does not dispute the complaint within 48 hours of being notified, a full refund of the tutor fee will be processed.
Complaints raised after 96 hours will still be reviewed, but the outcome is at Hello Tutor's discretion. Email us at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: Can I get a refund if I was unhappy with lesson quality?
items.answer: Yes. If you believe the quality of your lesson fell below a reasonable standard, raise a complaint within 96 hours of the lesson ending. Hello Tutor will review the situation and process a refund where appropriate.

items.question: What is the Hello Tutor Online Classroom?
items.answer: The Online Classroom is Hello Tutor's own built-in lesson space, available directly through the platform at app.hellotutor.me. All lessons take place here.

items.question: What equipment do I need?
items.answer: You will need a laptop, tablet, or desktop computer (recommended over mobile), a stable internet connection, and a working webcam and microphone. It is your responsibility to ensure everything is working before your lesson. No refund will be given if you cannot access the Online Classroom due to issues within your own control.

items.question: What if there is a technical problem during my lesson?
items.answer: If the issue is caused by something within your control, such as your internet connection or device, no refund will be given. If Hello Tutor's platform itself fails and this prevents a lesson from taking place, Hello Tutor will refund the Service Fee for that lesson. In either case, please report the issue to us at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How does Hello Tutor keep students safe?
items.answer:
The safety and wellbeing of all students, especially children, is our highest priority. Our safeguarding measures include:
  • Identity verification: government-issued photo ID and qualifications reviewed for every tutor.
  • Background checks: mandatory for all tutors, valid within the last three years.
  • Written references: two non-family references required for all tutors.
  • Lesson recording: all lessons are recorded and retained for safeguarding review.
  • Platform monitoring: messages and activity are monitored to prevent misuse.
  • Designated Safeguarding Officer: a named officer handles all concerns raised through the platform.
Hello Tutor cooperates fully with UAE law enforcement in connection with any safeguarding concern.

items.question: My child is under 18. What do I need to know?
items.answer: A parent or legal guardian must register the account and provide consent for any student under 18. As the responsible adult, you must not leave a child in the unsupervised sole care of a tutor during a lesson, must remain available so your child can report concerns without delay, and must ensure the lesson environment is appropriate and free from risk. By booking, you consent on the student's behalf to their lessons being recorded for safeguarding and quality purposes.

items.question: Can a parent or guardian sit in on a lesson?
items.answer: Yes. Parents are welcome to observe any lesson. Please let us know in advance so the tutor can plan accordingly.

items.question: Can a tutor cancel a lesson if no parent is present?
items.answer: Yes. Where a student is under 18 and no parent or legal guardian is available, a tutor may cancel the lesson if either the tutor or the student does not feel comfortable continuing. We strongly recommend that a parent or guardian is always reachable during lessons involving students under 18.

items.question: What happens when a safeguarding concern is reported?
items.answer: Any user reported for a safeguarding breach is immediately suspended pending investigation. Where suspected illegal activity is involved, the user may be permanently barred and reported to UAE law enforcement. Where a concern relates to a child, Hello Tutor will always prioritise that child's immediate safety and welfare above all other considerations.

items.question: How do I report a safeguarding concern?
items.answer: Contact Hello Tutor immediately at safeguarding@hellotutor.me if you have any concern about the safety or welfare of a student, or if you encounter anything inappropriate or illegal on the platform. All reports are handled by our Safeguarding Officer and treated as a priority.

items.question: What should I do if a tutor contacts me outside the platform?
items.answer: All communication between students and tutors must take place within Hello Tutor's platform. Tutors are not permitted to contact students or parents using personal details obtained through the platform. If you receive any unsolicited contact from a tutor outside the platform, report it immediately at safeguarding@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How is my personal data protected?
items.answer: Hello Tutor protects your personal information in accordance with our Privacy Policy and UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on the Protection of Personal Data. For privacy-related queries or to exercise your data rights, contact privacy@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How should I behave toward my tutor?
items.answer:
Students are expected to treat tutors with respect at all times — this means not subjecting tutors to abusive, offensive, or discriminatory behaviour or language, and not making any improper suggestions. If you are booking on behalf of a child, you are also responsible for ensuring the student behaves appropriately.

Any student who fails to meet these standards may be suspended from the platform.

items.question: What am I responsible for in terms of content and communications?
items.answer:
You are solely responsible for all content you post or send on the platform. You must not share anything that is defamatory, offensive, misleading, illegal, or that infringes a third party's intellectual property rights, and must not send unsolicited commercial messages to other users.

If you encounter any inappropriate or illegal content from another user, report it immediately at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: Does Hello Tutor offer group classes as well as 1-to-1 lessons?
items.answer: Yes. In addition to one-to-one lessons, Hello Tutor offers group classes delivered through the Online Classroom. They are recorded for safeguarding purposes and follow the same rules as individual lessons. You must be at least 18 to enrol. For students under 18, a parent or legal guardian must book and provide consent.

items.question: Can I cancel a group class enrolment?
items.answer:
You can cancel without charge if you give more than 72 hours' notice before the first lesson. Cancellations within that window are not eligible for a refund.

Missed lessons: no partial refunds are given for individual lessons within a class that you are unable to attend.

items.question: How do I update my personal or payment details?
items.answer: You can update your name, email address, password, notification preferences, and payment details at any time through your account settings. Please keep your contact details accurate, as Hello Tutor communicates with you by email and through the platform.

items.question: What if I think my account has been compromised?
items.answer: Contact Hello Tutor immediately at support@hellotutor.me if you believe your login credentials have been compromised. You remain responsible for any activity on your account until Hello Tutor has been notified.

items.question: Can Hello Tutor use images or content I upload to the platform?
items.answer: When you submit content to Hello Tutor, you grant us a licence to use that material for the purposes of operating and promoting the platform. Any images you independently create and upload may be used for marketing purposes without prior notice or compensation. Please bear this in mind when uploading images to your profile.

items.question: How will I be notified if the Terms or policies change?
items.answer: Hello Tutor will notify you of any changes to our Terms and Conditions at least 14 days before they take effect, via a notice on the platform or by email. Continued use of the platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

items.question: What happens to my data when I stop using Hello Tutor?
items.answer: You can stop using Hello Tutor at any time, subject to paying for any lessons already used. Hello Tutor retains certain data after your account is closed to meet its legal and safeguarding obligations, including lesson recordings stored for up to one year. To submit a data rights request, contact privacy@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How do I get in touch with Hello Tutor?
items.answer:
Reach us through the support section of the platform or by email:
  • General enquiries:support@hellotutor.me
  • Privacy and data rights:privacy@hellotutor.me
  • Safeguarding concerns:safeguarding@hellotutor.me
We acknowledge safeguarding reports immediately and aim to respond to general enquiries within 2 working days.

helpStrip.title: Still have a question?
helpStrip.body: Our team is here to help. Reach us through the platform or by email.

items.question: How does Hello Tutor work for parents?
items.answer: Hello Tutor handles everything on your behalf. You register, tell us about your child's learning needs, subject, level, and availability, and we find the right tutor, arrange the lessons, and manage payment. You are notified of every booking by email and through the platform.

items.question: How do I register on behalf of my child?
items.answer: You must be at least 18 years old to register an account. If the student is under 18, you register and manage the account as the parent or legal guardian, providing consent on your child's behalf for them to receive tuition. You remain the account holder responsible for all bookings and payments.

items.question: What should my child and I know before we start?
items.answer: Before using the platform, we recommend reading Hello Tutor's Safeguarding Policy with your child so they understand what appropriate behaviour looks like from both their tutor and themselves. Make sure your child knows they can come to you immediately if anything ever feels wrong during or after a lesson.

items.question: How are tutors vetted?
items.answer:
Before any tutor's profile goes live, Hello Tutor carries out thorough checks:
  • Identity verification: government-issued photo ID and qualifications are reviewed.
  • Background check: all tutors must hold a valid background check dated within the last three years. Tutors whose checks lapse are suspended immediately until the requirement is met.
  • Written references: two written references from non-family members are required.
  • UAE work or freelance permit: non-UAE national tutors must hold a valid permit from MOHRE or an equivalent free zone authority.
If you have a concern about a tutor, contact us before proceeding with a lesson.

items.question: Are tutors employed by Hello Tutor?
items.answer: No. Tutors on Hello Tutor are independent contractors, not Hello Tutor employees. When you confirm your first booking, you enter into a direct contract with the tutor for the delivery of lessons. Hello Tutor facilitates the match and provides the booking and payment infrastructure.

items.question: Should I also check my child's tutor myself?
items.answer: While Hello Tutor carries out checks on all tutors before approving them, you are also responsible for using your own judgement about a tutor's suitability. We recommend verifying any qualifications or experience claims before or at the start of your child's first lesson.

items.question: How are lessons booked for my child?
items.answer: Once you have confirmed your first booking, Hello Tutor will automatically arrange further lessons on your behalf. You will be notified by email and through the platform for every booking. Please always check that bookings are correct and share any relevant learning objectives or updates with Hello Tutor to keep lessons on track.

items.question: Can I sit in on my child's lesson?
items.answer: Yes. Parents are welcome to observe any lesson. Please let us know in advance so the tutor can plan accordingly.

items.question: Do I need to be available during my child's lesson?
items.answer: You must not leave a child under 18 in the unsupervised sole care of a tutor during any lesson. You do not need to sit in for the entire lesson, but you must remain close enough that your child can reach you immediately if they have any concerns.

items.question: Can a tutor cancel my child's lesson if I am not available?
items.answer: Yes. Where a student is under 18 and no parent or legal guardian is present or reachable, a tutor may cancel the lesson if either the tutor or the student does not feel comfortable continuing. Please always ensure you are reachable during your child's lessons.

items.question: Are my child's lessons recorded?
items.answer: Yes. All lessons are recorded by Hello Tutor for safeguarding and quality purposes. By booking and using the platform, you consent on your child's behalf to this recording. Recordings are stored for a minimum of 28 days and a maximum of one year, remain Hello Tutor's property, and may be reviewed by our safeguarding team or shared with UAE law enforcement if required.

items.question: Can my child contact their tutor outside the platform?
items.answer: No. All communication between students and tutors must take place through Hello Tutor's platform. You must ensure your child has no contact with their tutor outside the platform. Tutors are equally prohibited from contacting students or parents using personal details obtained through the platform.

items.question: What if my child's tutor does not show up?
items.answer: If the tutor does not join the Online Classroom within the first 15 minutes of the scheduled start time, you will not be charged. If the tutor cancels a confirmed booking, you will receive a full refund. Please raise a complaint within 96 hours of the scheduled lesson end time.

items.question: Can my child's tutor complete schoolwork or assignments for them?
items.answer: No. Tutors are strictly prohibited from completing or intending to complete coursework, essays, assignments, or similar academic work on a student's behalf. Any tutor found in breach of this may be removed from the platform.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: Hello Tutor operates on a subscription model. Your lesson fee is set by the subject, education level, and number of lessons in your subscription. All fees are shown before you subscribe and include any applicable UAE taxes. Payment is taken at the point of subscribing. All fees are displayed clearly before you confirm your subscription.

items.question: What does the lesson fee cover?
items.answer: Your lesson fee includes the Tutor Fee (paid to your child's tutor) and Hello Tutor's Service Fee (which covers matching, booking, and platform services). The Service Fee is included within the lesson fee and is not an additional charge.

items.question: Can I cancel a lesson?
items.answer:
You can cancel a booking at any point before the lesson starts, but cancellation charges apply:
  • More than 24 hours before the lesson: 50% of the lesson fee is charged.
  • Within 24 hours of the lesson: 100% of the lesson fee is charged.
Cancellations must be made through the platform. Once a lesson has started it cannot be cancelled. If your child does not attend without cancellation, the full fee is charged.

items.question: What if the tutor cancels?
items.answer: On rare occasions, a tutor may need to cancel due to unforeseen circumstances. If this happens, Hello Tutor will be in touch to reschedule the lesson as soon as possible. If rescheduling is not possible, you will receive a full refund.

items.question: How do I raise a complaint?
items.answer: Contact Hello Tutor by chat or email within 96 hours of the scheduled lesson end time. We will contact the tutor within 72 hours. If the tutor does not dispute the complaint within 48 hours of being notified, a full refund of the tutor fee will be processed. Email us at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: Are group classes available?
items.answer: Yes. Hello Tutor offers group classes as well as one-to-one lessons. For students under 18, you as the parent or guardian must book and provide consent. You can cancel enrolment without charge by giving more than 72 hours' notice before the first lesson. Cancellations within that window are not eligible for a refund, and no partial refunds are given for individual lessons missed within a class.

items.question: What are my safeguarding responsibilities as a parent?
items.answer:
As the parent or legal guardian of a student under 18, you have important safeguarding duties:
  • You are responsible for your child's welfare at all times during any lesson.
  • You must not leave your child in the unsupervised sole care of a tutor during a lesson.
  • You should remain available throughout lessons so your child can report any concerns to you without delay.
  • You are responsible for ensuring the lesson environment is appropriate and free from risk.
  • You must ensure your child treats their tutor with respect and is not subject to, or subjects the tutor to, abusive or inappropriate behaviour.
  • You must ensure your child has no communication with their tutor outside of the Hello Tutor platform.
  • We recommend reading Hello Tutor's Safeguarding Policy with your child before they start using the platform.

items.question: How does Hello Tutor safeguard my child?
items.answer: Hello Tutor takes the safety of all students, especially children, very seriously. Our safeguarding measures include identity verification, background checks (valid within the last three years), and two written references for every tutor before approval; recording of all lessons for safeguarding review, stored for up to one year; monitoring of platform messages and activity; a designated Safeguarding Officer for all concerns; and full cooperation with UAE law enforcement where required.

items.question: What should I do if a tutor contacts my child outside the platform?
items.answer: Tutors are not permitted to contact students or parents using personal details obtained through the platform. If you become aware of any unsolicited contact between a tutor and your child outside the platform, report it immediately at safeguarding@hellotutor.me.

items.question: How do I report a safeguarding concern?
items.answer: Contact Hello Tutor immediately at safeguarding@hellotutor.me. All reports are handled by our designated Safeguarding Officer. Where a concern relates to a child, Hello Tutor will always prioritise that child's immediate safety and welfare above all other considerations.

items.question: What happens when a safeguarding concern is reported?
items.answer: Any user reported for a safeguarding breach is immediately suspended from the platform pending investigation. Where suspected illegal activity is involved, the user may be permanently barred and reported to UAE law enforcement. Hello Tutor cooperates fully with any official investigation, including sharing lesson recordings and communications as required by law.

items.question: How is my family's data protected?
items.answer: Hello Tutor protects all personal information in accordance with our Privacy Policy and UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on the Protection of Personal Data. For privacy-related queries or to exercise data rights, contact privacy@hellotutor.me.

helpStrip.title: Still have a question?
helpStrip.body: Our team is here to help. Reach us through the platform or by email.

items.question: How do I join Hello Tutor as a tutor?
items.answer: You apply through the tutor onboarding process at app.hellotutor.me. Hello Tutor will review your application, verify your identity and credentials, and carry out the required checks before deciding whether to approve your profile. Your profile only goes live once Hello Tutor determines it is sufficiently complete and verified.

items.question: What checks are required before I can start tutoring?
items.answer:
Before your profile goes live, you must provide:
  • Government-issued identity document and evidence of relevant qualifications.
  • A valid background check dated within the last three years. If your check lapses while you are a registered tutor, your profile will be suspended immediately until you provide an updated one.
  • Two written references from individuals who are not family members, confirming their relationship to you and consenting to the reference being shared.
  • UAE work or freelance permit (non-UAE nationals only): a valid permit from MOHRE or an equivalent free zone authority. Failure to hold or maintain this permit will result in immediate suspension of your profile.
You must also disclose any criminal convictions or cautions at registration and notify Hello Tutor of any received afterwards for as long as you remain a registered tutor.

items.question: Am I employed by Hello Tutor?
items.answer: No. You are an independent contractor, not a Hello Tutor employee. You are solely responsible for your own tax obligations, professional insurance, and UAE permits. When a student confirms their first booking with you, they enter into a direct contract with you for the delivery of lessons.

items.question: Do I need to keep my profile information up to date?
items.answer: Yes. All information on your profile — including qualifications, subjects, rates, and personal statement — must be accurate, complete, and not misleading. Update this information promptly whenever it changes. Hello Tutor may suspend or remove profiles that contain inaccurate or misleading information.

items.question: How am I ranked on the platform?
items.answer: Tutor profiles are ranked based on profile completeness, student ratings, and number of lessons taught. Positive reviews, prompt responses to communications, and a higher volume of lessons delivered all contribute to a better ranking. Hello Tutor reserves the right to change the ranking methodology at any time.

items.question: How are my lessons booked?
items.answer: Hello Tutor arranges all bookings on behalf of students. Once a student confirms their first lesson with you, Hello Tutor will automatically arrange further lessons going forward. You will be notified of every booking through the platform. You must not attempt to arrange lessons directly with students outside the platform.

items.question: Can I arrange lessons directly with a student?
items.answer: No. All bookings must go through Hello Tutor. You must not use personal contact details obtained through the platform to arrange lessons outside of it.

items.question: What do I need to do before each lesson?
items.answer: Before each lesson, ensure you have reviewed any learning objectives or materials shared by Hello Tutor, your device, webcam, microphone, and internet connection are all working, and your lesson environment does not display any inappropriate images, materials, or documents that could be visible to students.

items.question: Where do lessons take place?
items.answer: All lessons are delivered through Hello Tutor's built-in Online Classroom at app.hellotutor.me.

items.question: Are lessons recorded?
items.answer: Yes. Hello Tutor records all lessons for safeguarding and quality purposes. Recordings are stored for a minimum of 28 days and a maximum of one year, and remain Hello Tutor's property. They may be reviewed where a complaint or safeguarding report has been made, and may be shared with UAE law enforcement if required. You must not independently record lessons by any means outside Hello Tutor's own recording system.

items.question: Can I cancel a confirmed lesson?
items.answer: Yes, but only where absolutely necessary. If you cancel a confirmed booking, the student will receive a full refund and the cancellation will be noted on your profile. Repeated cancellations may affect your ranking and Hello Tutor's willingness to continue matching students to you. Cancellations must be made through the platform.

items.question: Can I cancel a lesson if no parent is present for an under-18 student?
items.answer: Yes. Where a student is under 18 and no parent or legal guardian is available, you may cancel the lesson if either you or the student does not feel comfortable continuing. You are never obligated to proceed with a lesson involving a minor if appropriate supervision is not in place. Please notify Hello Tutor immediately if you cancel for this reason.

items.question: What if a student does not attend?
items.answer: If a student does not join the Online Classroom within the first 15 minutes of the scheduled start time, you will still be paid the Tutor Fee provided you were present and ready. Please remain in the Online Classroom for at least 15 minutes and notify Hello Tutor if the student does not appear.

items.question: Can I complete a student's coursework or essays for them?
items.answer: No. You are strictly prohibited from completing or intending to complete coursework, essays, assignments, or similar academic work on a student's behalf. Breach of this rule may result in immediate removal from the platform.

items.question: How is my fee determined?
items.answer: Hello Tutor sets all lesson fees based on the subject, education level, and the student's subscription. The total lesson fee is made up of the Tutor Fee (the portion you receive) and Hello Tutor's Service Fee (which covers the platform, matching, and payment infrastructure). Your Tutor Fee will be agreed with you as part of the onboarding process.

items.question: When do I receive payment?
items.answer: Hello Tutor collects payment from the student 24 hours after the scheduled end time of each lesson. Your Tutor Fee is then processed and paid to you on a schedule agreed during onboarding. Please ensure your payment details on your account are accurate and kept up to date.

items.question: What happens if a student's payment fails?
items.answer: If a student's payment fails, Hello Tutor will retry automatically at 48 and 72 hours after the lesson. If the payment remains outstanding at 96 hours, all future bookings for that student are cancelled. Hello Tutor manages the recovery process and will keep you informed where this affects your upcoming lessons.

items.question: Am I responsible for my own tax and insurance?
items.answer: Yes. As an independent contractor, you are solely responsible for meeting all applicable tax obligations arising from your earnings through Hello Tutor, and for taking out and maintaining your own professional insurance. Hello Tutor does not withhold tax on your behalf and is not responsible for your insurance arrangements.

items.question: Can Hello Tutor use images or content I upload to my profile?
items.answer: When you submit content to Hello Tutor, you grant us a licence to use that material for the purposes of operating and promoting the platform. Any images you independently create and upload may be used for marketing purposes without prior notice or compensation. Please bear this in mind when uploading images to your profile.

items.question: How must I conduct myself during lessons?
items.answer:
You must treat all students fairly and respectfully at all times. During lessons you must:
  • Use appropriate, professional language in all communications.
  • Confine lessons to the subject area for which you have been engaged.
  • Not make any improper or inappropriate suggestions to students.
  • Ensure your lesson environment is appropriate and free from any visible inappropriate material.

items.question: What are my responsibilities when teaching under-18 students?
items.answer: When teaching students under 18, you must work in partnership with their parent or legal guardian. You must ensure the parent or guardian has given consent for the student to receive tuition, and you may cancel a lesson if no parent or guardian is available and either you or the student does not feel comfortable continuing. You must take every reasonable precaution to protect younger students from harm.

items.question: What communication restrictions apply to me?
items.answer: All contact with students and parents must take place through Hello Tutor's platform. You must not contact students or parents using personal details obtained through the platform without their explicit consent and Hello Tutor's approval. Any unsolicited communication from a student outside the platform must be reported to Hello Tutor immediately.

items.question: What content rules apply to my profile and communications?
items.answer: You must not post, share, or transmit any defamatory, offensive, inappropriate, or illegal material through the platform, including in messages, profile content, or lesson communications. If you encounter any such material from another user, report it immediately at support@hellotutor.me.

items.question: What are my safeguarding duties?
items.answer:
You have a duty to protect the safety and welfare of all students, particularly those under 18. Your safeguarding responsibilities include:
  • Disclosing any criminal convictions or cautions at registration and at any point afterwards.
  • Disclosing any other relevant information that may lead Hello Tutor to determine you are not suitable to provide tutoring services.
  • Reporting immediately to Hello Tutor any concerns you have about a student's welfare or safety.
  • Ensuring all contact with students is appropriate to your role and confined to the subject area.
  • Reporting any inappropriate, offensive, or illegal material you encounter on the platform.

items.question: How do I report a safeguarding concern?
items.answer: Contact Hello Tutor immediately at safeguarding@hellotutor.me if you have any concern about the safety or welfare of a student, or if you encounter suspected illegal activity on the platform. All safeguarding reports are handled by our designated Safeguarding Officer and treated as a priority.

items.question: What happens if a complaint or safeguarding concern is raised about me?
items.answer:
If a complaint is raised, Hello Tutor will notify you within 72 hours of receiving it. You will have 48 hours to dispute it. If you do not respond within that time, Hello Tutor may process a full refund of the tutor fee to the student.

If a safeguarding concern or suspected illegal activity is reported, you will be immediately suspended from the platform pending investigation. Where illegal activity is involved, you may be permanently barred and reported to UAE law enforcement. Hello Tutor will cooperate fully with any official investigation, including providing access to lesson recordings.

items.question: Do I need to renew my background check?
items.answer: Yes. Your background check must have been issued within the last three years at all times. If it expires, your profile will be suspended immediately until you provide an updated one. It is your responsibility to renew before it lapses. Hello Tutor may request evidence of your background check status at any time.

items.question: How will I be notified if the Terms or policies change?
items.answer: Hello Tutor will notify you of any changes to our Terms and Conditions at least 14 days before they take effect, via a notice on the platform or by email. Continued use of the platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

items.question: How do I get in touch with Hello Tutor?
items.answer:
Reach us through the support section of the platform or by email:
  • General enquiries:support@hellotutor.me
  • Privacy and data rights:privacy@hellotutor.me
  • Safeguarding concerns:
We acknowledge safeguarding reports immediately and aim to respond to general enquiries within 2 working days.

helpStrip.title: Still have a question?
helpStrip.body: Our team is here to help. Reach us through the platform or by email.
